programmes.show_description: (2017) Drama starring Ruth Wilson. Following the death of her father, a woman returns home to Yorkshire to claim the tenancy of the family farm she believes is rightfully hers.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1954) Classic 'cowboys and Indians' Western. Gary Cooper, Richard Widmark and Cameron Mitchell star as three rascals hired by Susan Hayward to rescue her husband from Apache territory.
(1966) Classic sci-fi adventure. Raquel Welch and Donald Pleasence are among the crew of a miniaturised submarine that's injected into an injured diplomat's body in a bid to save his life.
(1956) Sci-fi thriller. Hugh Marlowe stars as a scientist who discovers Earth is threatened by a fleet of flying saucers bent on global conquest. With special effects by Ray Harryhausen.
(1955) Romantic drama with Jane Wyman and Rock Hudson. A wealthy widow decides to restart her life. But her children and her social set disapprove of her lover, forcing her to rethink.
(1999) Hugh Grant and Julia Roberts star in the BAFTA-winning comedy romance about a bookshop owner who manages to get a glamorous movie star to go out with him. With Rhys Ifans.
(2018) Acclaimed drama with Jessie Buckley and Julie Walters. In Glasgow, a young woman, fresh out of prison, plans to go to Nashville and become a Country and Western star.
(2020) Action thriller starring Liam Neeson as a notorious bank robber. He wants to give it all up for love but is cheated by corrupt FBI agents. Can he clear his name and get the girl?
(1999) Billy Connolly stars as a reformed criminal, now a famous artist, pursued by vengeful cop Ken Stott in Anthony Neilson's thriller.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1965) Thriller. Sidney Poitier plays a photojournalist aboard a destroyer whose commander's obsessive hunt for a Soviet sub could plunge the world into nuclear war.
Tom Hanks and director Paul Greengrass talk about making the tense, real-life thriller Captain Phillips.
(1957) Second World War drama. Deborah Kerr plays a nun stranded on a Japanese-held Pacific island, with an untalkative marine (Robert Mitchum) as her only companion and hope of salvation.

programmes.show_name: The True Story of Jesse James
programmes.show_description: (1957) Robert Wagner stars as the infamous outlaw in Nicholas Ray's western, which follows the last 18 years of the robber's life.
(1957) Second World War thriller. Robert Mitchum stars as the captain of a US destroyer in the North Atlantic hunting down a German U-boat and its world-weary captain, Curt Jurgens.
(2020) Comedy-drama with Liam Neeson. An estranged father and son team up to fix up and sell an old Tuscan villa that their late wife and mother left to them.
(2021) Action thriller with Bob Odenkirk. He's just a dad who goes to work and takes out the trash. But really he's an ex-assassin about to unleash his true self... and take out the trash.
(2017) BAFTA-winning psychological thriller with Jessie Buckley and Johnny Flynn. A fragile young woman begins a potentially dangerous relationship with a mysterious outsider.
